summary.intent: Refactor the gossamer Electron desktop app into a VS Code extension to reduce install friction (auto-detect .entire/settings.json instead of manual repo registration) and improve context-switching between editing code and viewing Claude Code sessions.
summary.outcome: A fully functional VS Code extension was built with sessions list, session detail view with compact full-width rows, native checkpoint tree sidebar, MCP server embedded at /mcp, QuickPick search with FTS and highlighting, image rendering, agent-aware labels and commands, onboarding flow, VSIX packaging script, and schema-based fresh-install initialization.

repo: Each repo gets its own SQLite DB at ~/.gossamer/repos/<name>.db; the top-level config is ~/.gossamer/config.json
Session data flows: Entire.io CLI → entire/checkpoints/v1 git branch + shadow branch → gossamer indexes into SQLite → VS Code extension displays
Custom session titles from /rename are stored as custom-title events only in ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/*.jsonl, NOT in the checkpoint or shadow branches
Session slugs on LogEvent are auto-generated and never change on rename; custom-title events are the actual rename signal
The .gossamer/checkpoints/ directory inside each repo is an intentional git worktree for the entire/checkpoints/v1 branch used by the indexer
Images are stored as base64 in the full.jsonl files on the entire/checkpoints/v1 branch and must be indexed into LogContent.imageData
The server runs from gossamer-testing/dist/serve.js (worktree) when launched via the extension dev host — edits to the main repo must be synced and rebuilt
Prisma CLI is 60MB+ and cannot be bundled in the VSIX; schema initialization must use raw SQL generated via 'prisma migrate diff --from-empty'

code.finding: startServer() calls syncLogContentFts(db) before tables exist on fresh DB, crashing the server before ensureRepoRegistered() can run — fixed by calling pushSchema(db) first
Server is a static singleton (static server, static serverPort) that outlives panel disposal, enabling minimize/restore without killing the server process
onDidChangeViewState listener needed to update checkpoint sidebar when switching between existing session detail tabs — without it sidebar only updates on first open
acquireVsCodeApi() can only be called once per webview lifetime — stored as window.__vscodeApi and reused in vscodeApi.ts singleton
Was only listing extension.ts and GossamerPanel.ts in include — SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts were never compiled, causing blank diff views at runtime
root: 'src/webview' was a relative path resolving from CWD (repo root when run via workspace), not package dir — must use resolve(__dirname, 'src/webview') with absolute path
Generated by 'prisma migrate diff --from-empty --to-schema' with sed to add IF NOT EXISTS; applied via raw SQL at startup instead of Prisma CLI — safe to re-run against existing DBs

workflow: gossamer-testing git worktree pattern: edit in gossamer/, sync changed files with cp, rebuild in gossamer-testing/ — the worktree has its own file copies that don't auto-sync
VS Code webview Content Security Policy requires explicit img-src data: https: http: to render base64 images
VS Code temp files approach for diffs was replaced by TextDocumentContentProvider with gossamer-diff: URI scheme — no disk writes, inherently read-only
For stable MCP config URLs, replaced getFreePort() random port selection with a configured DEFAULT_PORT=3456 stored in ~/.gossamer/config.json
File watchers (fs.watch) with debouncing (50ms DB, 200ms git refs) with 30s fallback polls are more responsive than fixed-interval polling for real-time updates
npm run build -w <workspace-name> requires the exact workspace name from package.json, not the directory name

friction: Server running from gossamer-testing/dist/serve.js (worktree) instead of main repo — stale builds were the root cause of multiple 'feature not working' bugs after changes were made in main repo
acquireVsCodeApi() called twice (inline minimize button script + React app) silently breaking the extension — had to store as window.__vscodeApi global
Prisma CLI approach to schema initialization doesn't work in bundled VSIX (no node_modules/.bin/prisma, can't use npx) — required generating schema.sql at build time
syncLogContentFts() crashing fresh server startup because tables don't exist yet, preventing ~/.gossamer from ever being created
Accidental Electron app changes when user asked to 'make the main gossamer window minimizable' — VS Code extension context was not maintained across the context boundary

The better-sqlite3 native module NODE_MODULE_VERSION mismatch (VS Code's Electron Node.js vs system Node.js) required spawning server as child process rather than importing Prisma directly in extension host
tab title showing wrong workspace because .vscode/launch.json had workspaceFolder as the args target instead of ~/gossamer
gossamer.vsix accidentally committed to git before user wanted it gitignored
open_items: Schema column additions (ALTER TABLE) not handled by the IF NOT EXISTS approach — future schema migrations will need explicit ALTER TABLE statements or a migration runner
Toggle panel keybinding (Ctrl+K G) doesn't fire when webview has keyboard focus — chord bindings are intercepted by the webview
Session color information from Claude Code is not persisted anywhere in the checkpoint/shadow branches — no way to show it
Named sessions feature (show slug instead of session ID) — intentionally tabled when user asked about it
Electron desktop app (app/) is broken/abandoned but still in the repo — should be removed or formally deprecated
The gossamer.vsix binary should be added to .gitignore along with packages/vscode/bundled-server/
gossamer-testing worktree sync is manual — could be automated with a watch script or Makefile target
MCP server's get_checkpoint_diff tool uses a separate diff-stats endpoint — could be combined for efficiency
packages/core/prisma/schema.sql must be manually regenerated when schema changes — no automated check in CI
